items.q: How many crotchets are in a semibreve?
items.why: Students confuse the relative durations. A semibreve (whole note) = 2 minims = 4 crotchets = 8 quavers. Each level halves in length.
Students confuse andante (walking, moderate) with adagio (slow). Andante literally means 'going/walking' in Italian - it's a comfortable pace, not slow.

items.q: How many semitones are in a perfect fifth?
items.why: Students count fifth as 'five steps' but the name refers to letter NAMES (1-2-3-4-5), not semitones. C to G spans 5 letter names but 7 semitones (C-C#-D-D#-E-F-F#-G).

items.q: Whose 1967 Treatise is a famous graphic score?
items.why: Correct answer: Cornelius Cardew. “John Cage” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year was MIDI standardised?
items.why: It's 1983. “1979” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Around what year did Les Paul pioneer multitrack recording?
items.why: The real figure is 1947 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
